file.content:
# Story 5.3: Cut `v1.0.0` under `--tag latest` with manual approval

Status: review

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

> **Precondition summary (2026-04-23 create-story time):** `v1.0.0-rc.3` is the passing RC on npm @rc (audit § Decision: PASS). `main` tip is [`3e60788`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/3e60788) (PR #212 merge — RC3 review patches). `jq -r .version package.json` on `main` returns `1.0.0-rc.3`; `jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` returns `1.0.0-rc.3`; `CHANGELOG.md` carries the auto-generated `## [1.0.0-rc.3]` entry (lines 8–395) AND the hand-curated `## [1.0.0] - TBD` placeholder (line 396). npm dist-tags: `{"latest":"0.10.0","alpha":"0.10.1-alpha.0","rc":"1.0.0-rc.3"}`. `release` GitHub Environment required-reviewer: `armelhbobdad`. Sprint-status: 5-3 is `backlog`; after create-story it flips to `ready-for-dev`.
>
> **Relationship to Stories 5.1 / 5.2 / 5.4:** Story 5.1 staged the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` placeholder and authored `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1`. Story 5.2 cut `v1.0.0-rc.3` through 7 dispatch attempts (surfacing 5 distinct pipeline defects that drove Stories 3-4 and 3-5) and signed off the audit with `## Story 5.2 RC Cut + Smoke Test`. Story 5.3 (this story) is the final promotion to `v1.0.0` under `--tag latest` — NFR6 immutability kicks in once `latest` points at `1.0.0`. Story 5.4 runs the cross-platform install verification within 1h of publish (Linux + Windows via `quality.yaml` matrix; macOS manual spot-check).

## Story

As the maintainer,
I want to cut `v1.0.0` only after an RC has survived clean smoke testing (Story 5.2 verified `v1.0.0-rc.3` passes) and I want the publish to pause for my explicit manual approval at the `release` environment gate,
so that the irreversible `--tag latest` publish represents a conscious decision — aligning with NFR6 (v1.0.0 is forever-burned once published under `latest`).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Two-commit scope envelope — Story 5.3 lands EXACTLY two commits on `main` (plus one workflow-authored release commit + one bot merge commit).** (a) **Commit 1: pre-dispatch verification commit — OPTIONAL, empty if no edits needed.** In the expected happy path, Story 5.3 has NO pre-workflow hand-edit requirement: unlike Story 5.2, `npm version major` on `1.0.0-rc.3` produces `1.0.0` without any 0.x→1.0.0 hand-bump — the semver transition is well-defined (see AC 2). If Task 1 recon surfaces an edge case that requires a pre-workflow fix (e.g., sprint-status 3-5 staleness that would cause the workflow to use an older buggy code path — exceedingly unlikely since 3-5 code is merged), the dev agent authors a targeted pre-dispatch commit and documents the scope expansion in Dev Agent Record. Otherwise, SKIP Commit 1 — proceed directly to AC 3 dispatch. (b) **Commit 2: post-workflow reconciliation + sign-off.** Subject `docs(release): reconcile CHANGELOG v1.0.0 and sign off publication (Story 5.3)`. Touches EXACTLY three files: `CHANGELOG.md` (reconcile duplicate `## [1.0.0]` blocks — see AC 10), `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` (append `## Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut` H2 at EOF — see AC 11), and `docs/RELEASING.md` (flip `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` section title + content to `### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest` — see AC 14, per `deferred-work.md` § 5-2 Round 2 line 132). **Workflow-authored commits** (NOT counted against Story 5.3's scope envelope): the single `release: bump to v1.0.0` commit that `release.yaml § Commit version bump` creates on the bot temp branch containing `package.json` + `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` + `CHANGELOG.md` edits, plus the merge commit from the bot PR auto-merge. **Verification:** `git log main --grep='Story 5.3' --oneline` returns Commit 2 subject (and Commit 1 if emitted); `git log main --grep='bump to v1.0.0$' --oneline` returns the workflow-authored commit (note: `$` anchor to disambiguate from `v1.0.0-rc.*`).

2. **`npm version major --no-git-tag-version` semver verification on a scratch clone — `1.0.0-rc.3` → `1.0.0`, NOT `2.0.0`.** Per node-semver's `semver.inc(ver, 'major')` behavior on a prerelease, `1.0.0-rc.3` strips the prerelease suffix and stays at the same major (since the prerelease is OF that major), producing `1.0.0`. Epic Story 5.3 AC explicitly warns about the alternative interpretation ("verify: `npm version major` on `1.0.0-rc.N` produces `1.0.0`, not `2.0.0-rc.0`"). **Task 1 verification (mandatory before dispatch):** in a scratch directory, `mkdir -p /tmp/skf-semver-check && cd /tmp/skf-semver-check && npm init -y && jq '.version = "1.0.0-rc.3"' package.json > /tmp/x && mv /tmp/x package.json && npm version major --no-git-tag-version && jq -r .version package.json` SHALL print `1.0.0`. If it prints `2.0.0` or `2.0.0-rc.0`, HALT and use the workflow-override alternative: file a Story 3.x follow-up to add explicit `npm version 1.0.0 --no-git-tag-version` override branch in `release.yaml § Bump version` for the major-from-rc case. **Expected outcome (per node-semver docs + empirical semver spec):** `1.0.0`. Clean up the scratch dir after verification.

3. **Pre-publish precondition check — read `package.json` version DIRECTLY from remote `main` (not local clone).** Per Story 5.2 AC 5 stronger-precondition-check pattern: `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/package.json --jq '.content' | base64 -d | jq -r .version` SHALL return `1.0.0-rc.3`. This short-circuits any local-clone staleness. If it returns anything other than `1.0.0-rc.3`: HALT and investigate — either Story 5.2's reconciliation was not committed, or another cut is in flight. Also confirm: `gh api .../contents/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json --jq '.content' | base64 -d | jq -r '.plugins[0].version'` → `1.0.0-rc.3`. Also confirm: `gh api .../contents/CHANGELOG.md --jq '.content' | base64 -d | grep -c '^## \[1\.0\.0-rc\.3\]'` → `1` (the rc.3 section is present). Also confirm: `gh api .../contents/CHANGELOG.md --jq '.content' | base64 -d | grep -c '^## \[1\.0\.0\] - TBD'` → `1` (the hand-curated placeholder survives for AC 10 reconciliation).

4. **`release.yaml` dispatched with `version_bump: major` — only from `main` tip.** After AC 2 + AC 3 pass: (a) `gh workflow run release.yaml -f version_bump=major --ref main` (the `--ref main` is load-bearing per Story 5.2 Dev Notes (D) — non-main dispatches take the legacy tag-only path at `release.yaml:639-647` which is NOT the canonical v1.0.0 cut); (b) capture the run id via `RUN_ID=$(gh run list --workflow=release.yaml --limit 1 --json databaseId --jq '.[0].databaseId')`; (c) monitor via `gh run watch $RUN_ID`; (d) the workflow pauses at TWO gates (Story 3.4 refactor): first the `release` environment gate at job start (Armel approves via UI to release the deployment), second the bot PR review-decision gate before auto-merge (Armel EITHER approves the bot PR via review UI OR admin-bypass-merges — per Story 5.2 pattern, admin-bypass-merge is acceptable and is the observed pattern for PRs #195/#196/#197/#209); (e) the workflow resumes after both gates, creates the tag on the merge commit post-merge, publishes to npm under `--tag latest`, creates the GitHub Release with `prerelease: false`, and emits the step summary; (f) final run conclusion SHALL be `success`.

5. **`release.yaml § Bump version` advances `1.0.0-rc.3` → `1.0.0`.** The case-chain at `release.yaml:113-115` routes `version_bump: major` → `npm version major --no-git-tag-version` (the `*) npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;` branch). On `package.json.version == "1.0.0-rc.3"`, this SHALL produce `1.0.0`. **Expected failure mode:** if `Bump version` produces `2.0.0` or errors, AC 2's scratch verification missed a semver edge case — HALT per AC 13 and fix via workflow override (see AC 2). **In-run verification:** monitor the `Get new version and previous tag` step (`release.yaml:118-122`) output — `echo "new_version=..."` SHALL emit `new_version=1.0.0` and `previous_tag=v1.0.0-rc.3`.

6. **Provenance attestation published under `--tag latest` (NFR4 100% provenance coverage).** After the workflow completes: `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 --json | jq '.dist.attestations'` SHALL return a non-null JSON object containing a `provenance.predicateType` of `https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1` (or `/v0.2` — both are SLSA L2; accept either). The attestation URL pattern SHALL match `https://registry.npmjs.org/-/npm/v1/attestations/bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0`. A `null` or absent attestation is a CRITICAL BLOCKER — v1.0.0 under `--tag latest` WITHOUT provenance is a direct NFR4 + NFR7 violation. If the attestation is absent, the ONLY acceptable recovery is to IMMEDIATELY `npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 "Critical: provenance absent; use v1.0.1 instead"` and cut v1.0.1 with provenance via a new `release.yaml` dispatch. Per NFR6, `npm unpublish` is NOT an option for v1.0.0 regardless of eligibility window. This is Scenario B of the Rollback Playbook (`docs/RELEASING.md § Scenario B`).

7. **Publish dist-tag is `latest`, NOT `rc`.** After the workflow completes: `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json` SHALL return `{"latest":"1.0.0","alpha":"0.10.1-alpha.0","rc":"1.0.0-rc.3"}` — **`latest` flips from `0.10.0` → `1.0.0`** (expected and INTENDED — this is the whole point of Story 5.3); `alpha` stays at `0.10.1-alpha.0` (unchanged); `rc` stays at `1.0.0-rc.3` (unchanged — Story 5.3's major-cut does NOT bump the rc tag). Also: `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@latest version` SHALL return `1.0.0`. Per `release.yaml:686-695` case-chain: the new version `1.0.0` contains none of `"alpha"` / `"beta"` / `"rc"` substrings, so falls through to the `else npm publish --tag latest` branch. **Expected failure mode (none anticipated):** if `latest` fails to flip to `1.0.0`, the case-chain substring match is broken — deferred-work.md § 5-2 F1 tracks substring-classification hardening as a post-v1.0.0 item; not Story 5.3 territory unless it actually fires.

8. **Workflow side-effects verified on `main` and at `v1.0.0` tag.** After the workflow completes, on `main`: (a) `main` tip advances by TWO commits per Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow (matching the rc.3 observation in Story 5.2 audit § Topology note) — the merge commit (bot PR auto-merged) + the `release: bump to v1.0.0` commit underneath; `git log main -1 --format='%H %s'` returns the merge commit; `git log main --grep='^release: bump to v1.0.0$' --oneline` returns the workflow-authored release commit (note `$` anchor to disambiguate from `v1.0.0-rc.*`); (b) `jq -r .version package.json` → `1.0.0` (AC 8b); (c) `jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` → `1.0.0` (AC 8c — SECOND live proof of Story 5.1 H3 jq-based marketplace sync working end-to-end after Story 5.2 rc.3 was the first); (d) `grep -cE '^## \[1\.0\.0\]' CHANGELOG.md` → `2` at this point (the auto-generated one from this cut + the stale `## [1.0.0] - TBD` placeholder). AC 10's reconciliation commit deletes the duplicate. (e) `grep -c '^## \[1.0.0-rc.3\]' CHANGELOG.md` → `1` (the rc.3 entry survives, as expected — workflow only prepends, awk preamble-restore doesn't touch older entries); (f) `head -5 CHANGELOG.md` SHALL show `# Changelog` at line 1 + preamble + `## [Unreleased]` (the Story 3.3 awk restore's preamble-preservation works identically to rc.3). And at the tag: (g) `git tag -l 'v1.0.0'` returns `v1.0.0` (note: exact match — NOT `v1.0.0-rc.*`); (h) `gh release view v1.0.0 --json tagName,isPrerelease,body,createdAt,htmlUrl` returns `{"tagName":"v1.0.0","isPrerelease":false,...}` — **`prerelease: false` is load-bearing** because `release.yaml:704` sets prerelease to true only when the version contains `alpha|beta|rc`; `1.0.0` contains none, so it's a stable release (NFR6 threshold).

9. **Tag anchors on the merge commit (Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow, not the CI-ephemeral release commit).** Per `release.yaml:649-684` (`Create and push tag` step with Story 3.4 P9 anchor-verification), under `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` the tag anchors on the PR merge commit (`$TAG_ANCHOR` comes from `gh pr view --json mergeCommit.oid`), NOT on the bot's release commit inside the temp branch. **Verification:** `git rev-parse v1.0.0` SHALL equal the merge commit SHA (the one at `main` tip after the bot PR auto-merges); `git log v1.0.0 -1 --format='%s'` SHALL return the merge commit subject (shape: `Merge pull request #<N> from armelhbobdad/release/bot/v1.0.0-<run_id>`), NOT the `release: bump to v1.0.0` subject. The underlying release commit is reachable via `git log v1.0.0 -2 --oneline` (second line). This topology was established in Story 5.2 for rc.3 (merge SHA `5fad470`; release commit `78ac999`).

10. **CHANGELOG v1.0.0 reconciliation in Commit 2 — resolve the TWO `## [1.0.0]` duplicates.** After the workflow completes, `CHANGELOG.md` has TWO `## [1.0.0]` H2 headers: (A) the auto-generated one at the top (position varies depending on awk restore: placed directly below `## [Unreleased]`, shape `## [1.0.0](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/v1.0.0-rc.3...v1.0.0) (2026-04-XX)`, containing conventional-commit-derived `### Features`/`### Bug Fixes` from commits between `v1.0.0-rc.3` and the new `v1.0.0` tag — likely sparse, mostly review-patch chores); (B) the hand-curated stale placeholder at the former line 396 (below `## [1.0.0-rc.3]` block), shape `## [1.0.0] - TBD` with the prose block authored by Story 5.1 (Features/Bug Fixes/Initial Release/Highlights/Workflows table/Tiers/IDE Support/Links). **Reconciliation protocol (Commit 2):** (i) REPLACE the auto-generated `## [1.0.0](...)` block's BODY (the `### Features` / `### Bug Fixes` sub-sections) with the hand-curated prose from block B (Initial Release through Links), preserving the auto-generated HEADER line (so the compare URL + date are verbatim what the workflow emitted — no risk of stale date); (ii) DELETE block B entirely (the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` header + all hand-curated content + any HTML comment preamble) — it has been merged into block A; (iii) post-edit verification: `grep -cE '^## \[1\.0\.0\]' CHANGELOG.md` → `1` (exactly one, the reconciled block); `grep -c '^## \[1\.0\.0\] - TBD' CHANGELOG.md` → `0` (stale placeholder gone); `grep -c '^## \[1\.0\.0\](https' CHANGELOG.md` → `1` (the reconciled block has the compare URL per Story 5.1's deferred-work line 117: "Story 5.3 final-cut reconciliation should add the `compare/v0.10.0...v1.0.0` URL when replacing `TBD` with the publish date"). Note: the compare URL IS in the auto-generated header; the reconciliation preserves it. (iv) Keep the `## [1.0.0-rc.3]` block untouched — it is historical and immutable once published.

11. **Audit artifact sign-off append.** Commit 2 modifies `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`: append a new H2 section `## Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut` AFTER `## Story 5.2 RC Cut + Smoke Test` (becomes the new bottom-most H2) containing these sub-sections: (i) `### Workflow dispatch run` — the `gh run view` URL + trigger / env-approval / pr-approval-or-admin-bypass-merge / completion timestamps + final `conclusion: success`; (ii) `### Bot PR auto-merge evidence` — the bot PR URL + merge-commit SHA on main + auto-merge vs admin-bypass-merge attestation (match the Story 5.2 rc.3 pattern — admin-bypass-merge is the expected path); (iii) `### npm publish evidence` — verbatim excerpted fields from `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 --json | jq '{version, dist: {tarball, shasum, attestations}}'`; pre-cut vs post-cut `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json` snapshots showing `latest: 0.10.0 → 1.0.0`, `rc: 1.0.0-rc.3` unchanged, `alpha: 0.10.1-alpha.0` unchanged; the updated `npm view versions --json` list (prior `["...","1.0.0-rc.3"]` now appended with `"1.0.0"`); (iv) `### GitHub Release v1.0.0` — verbatim output of `gh release view v1.0.0 --json tagName,isPrerelease,name,createdAt,htmlUrl,body` (the full body since v1.0.0 is the headline release — the hand-curated release notes are the record); (v) `### Main-branch side-effects` — the AC 8 (a)–(h) outputs verbatim + AC 9's tag-anchor verification; (vi) `### CHANGELOG reconciliation` — the `grep -c` counts from AC 10 (iii) + a before/after diff excerpt showing the merge of blocks A and B; (vii) `### NFR1 cycle-time record` — wall-clock delta for this dispatch (the 2nd data point in the rolling-10-release series per Story 5.2 audit § NFR1); (viii) `### NFR6 v1.0.0 immutability activation** — verbatim sentence: `v1.0.0 is live on npm under --tag latest as of <ISO-8601 UTC timestamp>. NFR6 immutability policy is now in force: this version SHALL NEVER be unpublished or republished; rollback uses npm deprecate + ship forward per docs/RELEASING.md § Scenario A/B.`; (ix) `### Decision: v1.0.0 launch status` — verbatim `LAUNCHED — v1.0.0 is live on npm under --tag latest with SLSA L2 provenance. Story 5.4 cross-platform verification cleared to begin (NFR9: within 1h of publish).` AND update the existing top-level `## Sign-off` block (line 199-201) to append a second sentence: `v1.0.0 launched 2026-MM-DD HH:MMZ; Story 5.4 cross-platform verification in scope.` (keep the Story 5.2 rc.3 sentence intact — this is an additive append).

12. **Quality gate (`npm run quality`) passes on Commit 2.** Before opening the PR for Commit 2: `npm run quality` SHALL exit 0 — all 13 subcommands green. Main failure modes for this specific commit: (a) `lint:md` — `CHANGELOG.md` + `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` + `docs/RELEASING.md` all run through markdownlint-cli2; any non-conforming heading shapes (ATX-style, one-space-after-hash) or broken table pipes in the reconciled v1.0.0 block will fail; (b) `validate:refs` — new links added to the audit artifact's sign-off section SHALL resolve to real commits/releases/runs on GitHub (the workflow run URL, the bot PR URL, the tag URL, the GitHub Release URL must all be reachable); (c) `lint:md` on `docs/RELEASING.md` — the `### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest` section replaces the prior `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` section; ensure the new section follows the same five-element shape (Trigger / CLI / Outcome / Constraints / Verification) as Scenarios A–G for consistency; (d) `prettier` — `CHANGELOG.md` is prettier-controlled; any hand-edits that drift from prettier's canonical form for markdown will fail. If any sub-check fails: iterate the edits until green BEFORE opening the PR (do NOT open a PR with local quality-gate failures — the required CI checks will block merge and burn a cycle).

13. **Blocker protocol — if Story 5.3 cannot proceed cleanly.** Mirror of Story 5.2 AC 21 logic with v1.0.0-specific intensification: if the workflow dispatch fails mid-run (any step exits non-zero), HALT and report — DO NOT retry-until-green on a failing workflow; investigate the failing step first. Given Story 5.2's 7-attempt saga already drove the workflow through 5 latent defects and fixed them, a Story 5.3 failure SHOULD be much rarer — but if one occurs, the defect-discovery protocol is: (a) file a same-repo GitHub issue with title `v1.0.0 release blocker: <one-line>`; (b) fix on a new feature branch → PR → merge to `main`; (c) **the re-dispatch produces a NEW version number** — this is where Story 5.3 differs from Story 5.2's rc.N iteration loop. A v1.0.0 cut that fails mid-pipeline AFTER the `Bump version` step runs has committed `1.0.0` to the temp branch; whether to re-dispatch as `1.0.0` or advance to `1.0.1` depends on the failure step: (i) failure BEFORE the bot PR auto-merges → temp branch is orphaned; delete it + re-dispatch with `version_bump: major` on `1.0.0-rc.3` → produces `1.0.0` again (same version, clean retry, recoverable per Scenario D of the Rollback Playbook); (ii) failure AFTER the bot PR auto-merges to main BUT BEFORE npm publish succeeds → `main` now has the `release: bump to v1.0.0` commit + `v1.0.0` tag pushed + NO npm artifact; this is Scenario D territory (`git tag -d v1.0.0 && git push --delete origin v1.0.0`); re-dispatch with `version_bump: patch` on `1.0.0` → produces `1.0.1` (the version number `1.0.0` is NOT burned on npm — `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0` returns 404 — so `1.0.0` could be reclaimed, BUT per NFR6 do not reclaim — ship `1.0.1` forward); (iii) failure AFTER npm publish succeeds but BEFORE GitHub Release creation → `v1.0.0` is live on npm with provenance; this is Scenario E (`gh release create v1.0.0 ...` manual recovery) — the published artifact stands, manual reconciliation completes the release. Per NFR6, once `npm publish --tag latest` succeeds for `1.0.0`, the version is IMMUTABLE — no retry, no re-cut, no unpublish. If failure is outside Story 5.3's fix scope (systemic npm outage, GitHub OIDC regression), mark as external-blocker, pause, and resume when upstream is healthy.

14. **`docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` → `§ Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest` title + content flip in Commit 2** (per `deferred-work.md § 5-2 Round 2` line 132). The existing section (`docs/RELEASING.md:559-616`) documents the hand-bump + rc-cut pattern that Stories 5.1 and 5.2 used; it is single-use historical documentation of a one-time event. Story 5.3 replaces it with a `### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest` section documenting the final promotion path, same five-element shape: (a) **Trigger.** RC survived clean smoke test (`release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.2 RC Cut + Smoke Test § Decision`: `PASS`); `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@rc version` returns the passing RC; Story 5.3 ready to promote to `latest` under manual approval gate. (b) **CLI.** `gh workflow run release.yaml -f version_bump=major --ref main` (dispatches from `main` tip; `--ref main` load-bearing per Story 5.2 D); monitor via `gh run watch`; approve at the `release` env gate; approve OR admin-bypass-merge the bot PR; workflow auto-tags, publishes under `--tag latest`, creates GitHub Release. Expected ~5-8 min wall-clock if gates approved quickly. (c) **Expected outcome.** `main` tip advances by 2 commits (merge + release); `jq -r .version package.json` → `1.0.0`; `jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` → `1.0.0`; `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags.latest` → `1.0.0`; `rc` dist-tag UNCHANGED at the prior RC; SLSA L2 provenance attached; GitHub Release `v1.0.0` with `prerelease: false`. (d) **Constraints.** Execute ONLY after RC audit Sign-off is populated AND the smoke-test Decision is `PASS`. The cut is IRREVERSIBLE — once `npm publish --tag latest` succeeds, NFR6 forbids unpublish regardless of eligibility window; rollback is `npm deprecate` + ship forward (Scenario A/B). The CHANGELOG reconciliation is a POST-workflow step (Commit 2); do NOT hand-edit the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` placeholder BEFORE dispatch — let the workflow auto-generate its block, then reconcile in one commit. (e) **Verification.** Include the AC 6 / AC 7 / AC 8 / AC 10 verification greps + the NFR6 immutability activation timestamp. Keep the existing five-element-shape scaffolding that Story 5.1 authored. Remove Story 5.1's pre-Story-3.4 prose about the non-PR-auto-merge flow (the "Story 3.4 refactor note — PR-auto-merge flow" appendix at `docs/RELEASING.md:614-616` can be absorbed into the main section body since PR-auto-merge is now the canonical flow).

15. **Commit hygiene on Commit 2.** Subject: `docs(release): reconcile CHANGELOG v1.0.0 and sign off publication (Story 5.3)` (64 chars — under the 72-char limit). Body requirements: (a) reference the workflow run URL absolutely; (b) reference the bot PR URL + merge commit SHA; (c) reference the GitHub Release `v1.0.0` URL; (d) list the three files touched (`CHANGELOG.md`, `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`, `docs/RELEASING.md`) with per-file purpose one-liners; (e) NO `Fixes #NN` trailer (per user-memory `feedback_no_issue_numbers_in_pr.md`); (f) NO `_bmad-output/todo/` references; (g) `AC N` reference form (NOT `#N`) if ACs are cited; (h) one short paragraph describing the CHANGELOG reconciliation decision (merging blocks A + B into a single reconciled entry); (i) commit-msg + pre-commit hooks pass without `--no-verify`.

16. **Branch-protection compliance — Commit 2 goes through PR, not direct push.** Per Story 5.1 audit Row 9 + Story 5.2 AC 17: direct push to `main` is blocked. Commit 2 is authored on a fresh branch `feat/v1-final-signoff` (fresh from `origin/main` tip AFTER the workflow-authored release commits land — wait for the bot PR auto-merge to complete BEFORE creating this branch), pushed via PR, gated by all 7 required status checks + review approval (self-approve or admin-bypass-merge, matching the rc.3 pattern for PRs #210 + #212).

17. **Sprint-status update at completion.** After Commit 2 is merged to `main`: update `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml` — flip `5-3-cut-v1-0-0-under-tag-latest-with-manual-approval` from `in-progress` → `review` (by dev-story at Commit 2 branch creation) → `done` (by code-review once approved). This follows the standard Dev → Review → Done flow; Story 5.3's dev-story phase updates the status per standard sprint-tracking conventions. Note: Epic 5 status remains `in-progress` at Story 5.3 completion — Story 5.4 is still backlog; Epic 5 flips to `done` only after 5.4 closes (per sprint-status header rules).

18. **NFR6 immutability annotation — audit artifact captures the policy activation.** Per AC 11 (viii): the audit artifact's `## Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut § NFR6 v1.0.0 immutability activation` subsection records the verbatim timestamp at which `npm publish --tag latest` succeeded. From that instant: any future defect in `1.0.0` is resolved via `npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 "..."` + shipping `1.0.1` forward — NEVER `npm unpublish`. This annotation is the forensic record that the policy activation was deliberate, witnessed by an approver, and timestamped for audit replay.

19. **Runtime / npm floor reconfirmation (NFR8 reconfirm).** The `release.yaml § Verify npm version floor for OIDC trusted publishing` step at lines 80-93 is the runtime enforcement (npm ≥11.5.1). `.nvmrc` pins Node to `22` (LTS channel; GitHub Actions `setup-node` resolves to latest 22.x, which is ≥22.14.0). The workflow self-asserts; no Story 5.3 action required BEYOND verifying that the dispatch succeeds through that step. Pre-dispatch check: `cat .nvmrc` SHALL return `22` (or a value ≥22.14.0).

20. **Zero new files created in Story 5.3.** Unlike Story 5.1 (created `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`, later moved to `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`), Story 5.3 creates ZERO new files. It MODIFIES three files in Commit 2 (`CHANGELOG.md`, `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`, `docs/RELEASING.md`). Workflow-side-effect modifications on `package.json`, `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, `CHANGELOG.md` (auto-generated block) are NOT Story 5.3 file-scope. **Verification:** `git log main --grep='Story 5.3' --name-only --format='%H %s'` enumerates exactly Commit 2's file list; no `A <path>` (added) entries.

---

# Step 2: Review

## RULES

- YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
- The Blind Hunter subagent receives NO project context — diff only.
- The Edge Case Hunter subagent receives diff and project read access.
- The Acceptance Auditor subagent receives diff, spec, and context docs.

## INSTRUCTIONS

1. If `{review_mode}` = `"no-spec"`, note to the user: "Acceptance Auditor skipped — no spec file provided."

2. Launch parallel subagents without conversation context. If subagents are not available, generate prompt files in `{implementation_artifacts}` — one per reviewer role below — and HALT. Ask the user to run each in a separate session (ideally a different LLM) and paste back the findings. When findings are pasted, resume from this point and proceed to step 3.

   - **Blind Hunter** — receives `{diff_output}` only. No spec, no context docs, no project access. Invoke via the `bmad-review-adversarial-general` skill.

   - **Edge Case Hunter** — receives `{diff_output}` and read access to the project. Invoke via the `bmad-review-edge-case-hunter` skill.

   - **Acceptance Auditor** (only if `{review_mode}` = `"full"`) — receives `{diff_output}`, the content of the file at `{spec_file}`, and any loaded context docs. Its prompt:
     > You are an Acceptance Auditor. Review this diff against the spec and context docs. Check for: violations of acceptance criteria, deviations from spec intent, missing implementation of specified behavior, contradictions between spec constraints and actual code. Output findings as a Markdown list. Each finding: one-line title, which AC/constraint it violates, and evidence from the diff.

3. **Subagent failure handling**: If any subagent fails, times out, or returns empty results, append the layer name to `{failed_layers}` (comma-separated) and proceed with findings from the remaining layers.

4. Collect all findings from the completed layers.


## NEXT

Read fully and follow `./step-03-triage.md`

    docs(release): reconcile CHANGELOG v1.0.0 and sign off publication (Story 5.3)
    
    Story 5.3 promoted the passing v1.0.0-rc.3 RC to v1.0.0 under --tag latest
    via release.yaml run 24852899833 (dispatched 2026-04-23T18:49:47Z, completed
    2026-04-23T18:56:48Z — 7m 1s, well within the NFR1 20-min envelope).
    v1.0.0 is live on npm with SLSA L2 provenance. NFR6 immutability activated
    at 2026-04-23T18:56:39Z — v1.0.0 is now forever-burned under --tag latest;
    rollback from this instant is npm deprecate + ship-forward only.
    
    This sign-off commit is the post-workflow reconciliation referenced by AC 1b,
    AC 10, AC 11, and AC 14 of Story 5.3. The release.yaml workflow landed the
    auto-generated v1.0.0 CHANGELOG block, the marketplace.json jq sync, and the
    package.json version bump as workflow-authored commits (bot PR #213 merge
    SHA 393a5a2). This commit reconciles the post-workflow state.
    
    CHANGELOG reconciliation decision: the workflow's conventional-changelog
    preset emitted an empty ## [1.0.0] block (zero feat: / fix: entries between
    rc.3 and v1.0.0 — the intermediate commits were only docs(release): and
    chore(release): sign-off work). Rather than discard the Story 5.1 hand-curated
    prose staged at ## [1.0.0] - TBD, this commit preserves the auto-gen header
    line (compare URL + publish date) and injects the hand-curated body (First
    Major Release / Initial Release / Highlights / Workflows / Confidence Tiers /
    IDE Support / Links), then deletes the stale TBD placeholder block. Net: one
    reconciled ## [1.0.0] entry aligned with the sibling shape of prior releases.
    
    Files touched:
    
    - CHANGELOG.md — AC 10 reconciliation: merge the auto-gen ## [1.0.0] compare
      URL header with the hand-curated prose from the stale TBD placeholder; delete
      the placeholder block entirely. Post-edit: exactly one ## [1.0.0] H2, zero
      ## [1.0.0] - TBD entries, rc.3 block untouched, older history intact.
    
    - release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md — AC 11 / AC 18 sign-off append:
      new ## Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut H2 at EOF with 9 sub-sections (workflow
      dispatch run, bot PR evidence, npm publish evidence, GitHub Release, main
      side-effects, CHANGELOG reconciliation, NFR1 cycle-time record, NFR6
      immutability activation, Decision: LAUNCHED); ## Sign-off block extended
      to dual-sentence form recording the 18:56:39Z NFR6 activation timestamp.
    
    - docs/RELEASING.md — AC 14 section flip: ### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1 replaced
      with ### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest, documenting the two-gate
      maintainer-approval promotion path (release env + bot PR approval-or-admin-
      bypass-merge) as the canonical major-cut procedure. Absorbs the former Story
      3.4 appendix into the main section body — PR-auto-merge is now the default
      flow for main-dispatched cuts.
    
    Key references:
    
    - Workflow run: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24852899833
    - Bot PR #213: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/213
      (merge commit 393a5a2012e8914faa3f67e1af94bc3ca0c90062, admin-bypass-merged
      2026-04-23T18:56:30Z)
    - GitHub Release v1.0.0: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v1.0.0
    - npm: bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 under dist-tag latest with SLSA L2 provenance
    
    NFR1 rolling-10-release window: 2 / 10 data points collected — [Story 5.2
    rc.3: 5m 4s, Story 5.3 v1.0.0: 7m 1s]. Story 5.3's first-attempt success
    validates that the pipeline defects surfaced during Story 5.2's 7-attempt
    saga (Stories 3-4, 3-5, PRs #205 / #207) are fully resolved.
    
    Story 5.4 (cross-platform install verification within NFR9's 60-min window
    from publish) is cleared to begin.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: bff07d35c951

input.prompt:
You are the **Blind Hunter** — an adversarial code reviewer.

**Strict context rules (non-negotiable):**
- You receive ONLY a unified diff. You have NO project context, NO spec, NO access to any project files.
- You MAY read the single file at `/tmp/story-5-3-diff.patch` (the diff) — nothing else.
- You MUST NOT read, glob, or grep any files in `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/` or any other project directory.
- You MUST NOT fetch URLs or search the web.
- Base your review solely on what the diff text itself reveals.

**Task:** Invoke the `bmad-review-adversarial-general` skill to perform a Cynical Review of the diff at `/tmp/story-5-3-diff.patch`.

The diff is Commit 2 of Story 5.3 of the `bmad-module-skill-forge` project — a documentation-heavy reconciliation commit that modifies three markdown files:
- `CHANGELOG.md` — reconciles a v1.0.0 entry
- `docs/RELEASING.md` — replaces a section on cutting RC releases with a section on cutting v1.0.0 under `--tag latest`
- `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` — appends a sign-off section recording a v1.0.0 npm publish

That is all the context you get. Attack the diff on its own merits: internal consistency, claim vs. evidence, contradictions, incoherent prose, broken markdown, fabricated details, and anything that doesn't add up when you read it skeptically.

**Output:** A Markdown findings list. Each finding must include:
- One-line title
- Severity (CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW)
- Concrete evidence quoted from the diff (with file + line hint)
- Why it matters

If you find nothing worth flagging at a given severity, say so explicitly — do not pad.

You are the **Edge Case Hunter** — an exhaustive boundary-condition reviewer.

**Context:**
- Project root: `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge`
- Diff to review: `/tmp/story-5-3-diff.patch` (Story 5.3 Commit 2 — 3 files, ~354 lines added, ~105 removed)
- You MAY read any project files to verify claims, cross-check references, and trace edge cases.
- You MAY NOT modify any files.

**Task:** Invoke the `bmad-review-edge-case-hunter` skill against the diff at `/tmp/story-5-3-diff.patch`.

This is a documentation reconciliation commit for a v1.0.0 npm release. It modifies:
- `CHANGELOG.md` — merges an auto-generated v1.0.0 block with hand-curated prose; deletes a stale `## [1.0.0] - TBD` placeholder
- `docs/RELEASING.md` — replaces `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` with `### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest`; absorbs a prior Story 3.4 appendix
- `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` — appends `## Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut` H2 with 9 sub-sections; extends existing `## Sign-off` block with a second sentence

**Focus areas (walk every branch, not just happy path):**
1. **Claim verifiability** — every URL, SHA, version number, timestamp, and dist-tag claim in the diff: does it match what's on disk / reachable? Pick a sample and verify.
2. **Markdown structural edges** — heading hierarchy, broken tables, unclosed code fences, link anchors that point nowhere, nested-list indentation that breaks under the markdownlint rules this repo uses (check `.markdownlint*` and `npm run lint:md` config).
3. **CHANGELOG reconciliation edges** — could the merge of the auto-gen header with hand-curated prose leave orphaned fragments? Is the `## [Unreleased]` section still intact? Are compare URLs internally consistent?
4. **Cross-doc consistency** — does `docs/RELEASING.md`'s new section contradict any claim in the audit artifact? Do file paths / commands referenced in the new content still exist in the repo?
5. **Boundary conditions in the release flow claims** — the diff describes what "happened" during the release; spot-check the release workflow at `.github/workflows/release.yaml` to see whether the narrative matches the actual workflow step ordering / logic.
6. **Removed-content gotchas** — the `docs/RELEASING.md` edit removes a Story 3.4 appendix; does anything else in the repo still reference the removed anchors or prose?

**Output:** A Markdown findings list. Each finding:
- One-line title
- Severity (CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW)
- Specific file:line citation + concrete evidence
- Why it matters (what could break / what reader is misled)

Skip the happy path. Report only unhandled edges. Don't pad — if a focus area is clean, say so.

You are an **Acceptance Auditor**.

**Context:**
- Project root: `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge`
- Diff to review: `/tmp/story-5-3-diff.patch`
- Spec file: `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/5-3-cut-v1-0-0-under-tag-latest-with-manual-approval.md` (Story 5.3 spec — 20 acceptance criteria)
- You MAY read any project files to verify claims. You MAY NOT modify files.

**Task:** Review the diff against the spec. Check for:
- Violations of acceptance criteria (AC 1–20)
- Deviations from spec intent
- Missing implementation of specified behavior
- Contradictions between spec constraints and actual code/content
- Commit-hygiene rule violations (AC 15)
- Scope envelope violations (AC 1: commit must touch EXACTLY three files: `CHANGELOG.md`, `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`, `docs/RELEASING.md`)

**Priority ACs to audit closely:**
- **AC 1b** — Commit 2 subject line + exactly-three-files scope
- **AC 10** — CHANGELOG v1.0.0 reconciliation protocol (merge header of auto-gen block A with body of hand-curated block B; delete block B entirely; post-edit grep counts)

content.text:
Now I have the complete diff. Let me perform the adversarial review.

# Cynical Review Findings — /tmp/story-5-3-diff.patch

## 1. Timestamp contradiction: sign-off dated BEFORE the event it records

**Severity: HIGH**

The Sign-off line (launch-audit.md:201) reads:

> "Signed off by Armel (@armelhbobdad) at **2026-04-23 16:40Z** (post-publish, after smoke test completion). v1.0.0-rc.3 survived smoke test clean; Story 5.3 unblocked. **v1.0.0 launched 2026-04-23 18:56:39Z**; Story 5.4 cross-platform verification in scope."

The sign-off timestamp (16:40Z) is **over two hours earlier** than the v1.0.0 launch it references (18:56:39Z). A sign-off cannot "record" an event that has not yet occurred. Either the sign-off must be re-dated, a second sign-off line appended with a post-launch timestamp, or the sentence clause restructured to say "subsequent to this sign-off, v1.0.0 was launched…". As written, the prose is literally time-travelling.

---

## 2. Self-referential broken cross-reference in the HTML comment (now deleted — but the RELEASING.md rewrite keeps the dangling pointer)

**Severity: MEDIUM**

The deleted HTML comment (CHANGELOG.md, old lines 72–76) said the reconciliation procedure was documented at `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1`. That section heading has been **renamed in the same diff** to `### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest` (RELEASING.md:559). Any other file in the repo that links to `#cutting-v100-rc1` is now broken. The diff deletes the pointer on one side but gives zero evidence the other side was swept. At minimum there is a latent risk; more likely there are other stale references that weren't audited.

---

## 3. Auto-generated release-body claim contradicts listed commit-range content

**Severity: MEDIUM**

In launch-audit.md:

> "Release body: auto-generated by `release.yaml § Generate release notes` from the conventional-commits between `v1.0.0-rc.3` and `v1.0.0` (**expected sparse** given the narrow commit-range…)"

A few paragraphs later the audit states:

> "the commit range `v1.0.0-rc.3..v1.0.0` contained only `docs(release):` and `chore(release):` entries."

If the only entries are `docs:` and `chore:`, the body is not merely "sparse" — it is **empty** (no `### Features` / `### Bug Fixes` under standard conventional-commit release-note generation, which is exactly what the audit admits one section later for the CHANGELOG block). Calling the GitHub Release body "sparse" while calling the CHANGELOG block "EMPTY" for the same commit range is inconsistent wording. One of them is wrong or the two generators do different things — the diff does not say which.

---

## 4. "AC 6 NFR4 provenance-coverage status: PASS" — evidence doesn't fully match the stated check

**Severity: MEDIUM**

The Verification block in RELEASING.md:232–238 sets the expectation:

> `.provenance.predicateType` starts with `"https://slsa.dev/provenance/"`

The audit's captured `.dist` object (launch-audit.md) shows:

```
"predicateType": "https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1"
```

Fine so far. But the RELEASING.md prose also claims **SLSA L2**, and the evidence block captures only a `predicateType` URL — there is **no captured evidence of the attestation bundle's signing level (L1 vs L2 vs L3)**. SLSA level is not encoded in `predicateType`; `https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1` is a predicate schema version, not a level. Claiming "SLSA L2" as a fact based on this JSON is unsupported by the evidence shown. The audit asserts L2 four times without ever capturing the underlying proof.

---

## 5. Internal contradiction: bot-PR-merge step description

**Severity: MEDIUM**

launch-audit.md says:

> "Bot PR approval-or-admin-bypass merge at: `2026-04-23T18:56:30Z` (admin-bypass-merge — the PR's `Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge` step cleared in 0s **because the merge had already happened by the time that step evaluated**…"

If the merge "had already happened" when the step evaluated, **who merged it**? The workflow step is the thing that gates on merge approval; if the merge happened before the step ran, something else merged the PR outside the workflow's control. The prose treats this as business-as-usual ("matches the rc.3 pattern"), but the causal chain as described is: maintainer merged via GitHub UI → workflow step ran → step saw `merged=true` → step exited 0. That is a **race-condition-friendly design** hand-waved as "admin-bypass-merge." The diff asserts the pattern is approved-by-design but provides no citation beyond "matches rc.3."

---

## 6. "Main-dispatched cuts" — two-gate claim doesn't match the observed single-gate clearance

**Severity: MEDIUM**

RELEASING.md:586 states the cut requires approval **at two gates**:

> "1. The `release` environment deployment gate… 2. The bot PR review-decision gate (after the 7 required status checks pass)."

But launch-audit.md shows only one explicit approval event (the env-gate at 18:52:32Z); the "second gate" was cleared via admin-bypass-merge at 18:56:30Z. That is **not an approval**, that is a bypass. The canonical flow as documented says "approve… OR admin-bypass-merge — both paths are accepted," but the prose earlier ("approval is required at **two** gates") flatly says "approval is required." Pick one: is approval required at both gates, or is bypass equivalent to approval at the second? The docs disagree with themselves within ten lines of each other.

---

## 7. `npm version major` semver claim is presented as fact without citation

**Severity: MEDIUM**

RELEASING.md:571–574:

> "`version_bump: major` on 1.0.0-rc.N strips the prerelease suffix and produces 1.0.0 (node-semver inc(ver, 'major') behavior on a prerelease)."

And at RELEASING.md:593:

> "If `npm version major` unexpectedly emits `2.0.0` instead of `1.0.0` on `1.0.0-rc.N`, the node-semver engine behavior has regressed."

This is a non-trivial semver assertion — node-semver's `inc(ver, 'major')` on `1.0.0-rc.3` is documented to return `1.0.0` (prerelease strip), but asserting it as load-bearing operational behavior warrants at minimum a link to the node-semver docs or a captured dry-run. The diff states this as settled fact and then instructs the reader to abort if it behaves differently — a reader who doesn't already know the semver spec has no way to verify the claim from the document itself.

---

## 8. "Zero cache miss" in NFR1 calculation arithmetic

**Severity: LOW**

launch-audit.md NFR1 section:

- Dispatch: `2026-04-23T18:49:47Z`
- Completion: `2026-04-23T18:56:48Z`
- Claim: **7m 1s** wall-clock

18:56:48 − 18:49:47 = **7m 1s** ✓ arithmetic is fine.

- Env-gate approval: "2m 45s" (18:49:47 → 18:52:32)
- 18:52:32 − 18:49:47 = **2m 45s** ✓
- Workflow automation: "4m 16s" (18:52:32 → 18:56:48)
- 18:56:48 − 18:52:32 = **4m 16s** ✓

So the arithmetic is actually consistent — but note: **2m 45s + 4m 16s = 7m 1s** exactly, which assumes the two sub-budgets are contiguous with no gap. The audit presents this as fact without evidence, and the publish-timestamp (18:56:39Z) sits inside the "workflow automation" window. If the workflow truly completed at 18:56:48Z but published at 18:56:39Z, there are **9 seconds of post-publish step execution** unaccounted for. Not wrong, but unexplained in a document that otherwise goes to forensic precision about timestamps.

---

## 9. "Rolling-10-release window" data is cherry-picked

**Severity: LOW**

> "Rolling-10-release window: 2 / 10 data points collected — `[Story 5.2 rc.3: 5m 4s, Story 5.3 v1.0.0: 7m 1s]`."

A rolling-10 window is claimed but the audit drops 8 of 10 slots. Worse — Story 5.2 is described elsewhere in this very diff as **"7 attempts" over "~6 hours"**. Including only the final successful 5m 4s attempt in the NFR1 metric, while the broader narrative leans on the 6-hour cost, is selection bias. NFR1's "20 min (15 CI + 5 approval)" target is trivially met if you count only the successful attempts; if you count all dispatches, rc.3 alone blew past the target ~20x over. The diff does not declare how failed dispatches count toward the rolling window.

---

## 10. Markdown: unclosed table-like list in the "Workflows" section

**Severity: LOW**

CHANGELOG.md "Workflows" table lists **14 workflows** in its rows, matching the bullet "**14 workflows**" above. Good. But the bullet earlier says:

> "**1 agent** — Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian) with 5 workflow-driven modes"

5 modes, 14 workflows. These numbers are not reconciled anywhere in the diff. What is a "mode" vs a "workflow"? Is a mode a subset, superset, or orthogonal concept? The release notes assert both numbers with authority and let the reader guess.

---

## 11. "IDE Support — 23 IDEs" headline number does not match the comma-separated list

**Severity: HIGH**

CHANGELOG.md:55:

> "23 IDEs supported: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, Junie, Kiro, Trae, Google Antigravity, Auggie, CodeBuddy, Crush, iFlow, KiloCoder, Ona, OpenCode, Pi, Qoder, QwenCoder, Rovo Dev"

Count: Claude Code (1), Cursor (2), Windsurf (3), Cline (4), Roo Code (5), GitHub Copilot (6), Codex (7), Gemini CLI (8), Junie (9), Kiro (10), Trae (11), Google Antigravity (12), Auggie (13), CodeBuddy (14), Crush (15), iFlow (16), KiloCoder (17), Ona (18), OpenCode (19), Pi (20), Qoder (21), QwenCoder (22), Rovo Dev (23).

OK — **23 matches**. I was primed to find a mismatch and there isn't one. Finding withdrawn. Replacing with:

**Replacement 11 — Highlights self-contradiction on "14" count**

> "**14 workflows** — full lifecycle from source analysis…"
> "**14 knowledge fragments** — curated cross-cutting principles…"

Two different categories coincidentally both number exactly 14. That's not intrinsically wrong, but the phrasing reuse ("14 X, 14 Y") reads like a template that was partially filled, and a cynical reader will assume at least one of those counts is copy-paste inertia until the diff provides evidence otherwise. The diff provides no enumeration of the 14 knowledge fragments — only the 14 workflows are enumerated in the table below. One of the two "14s" is verifiable from the diff content; the other is an unverified claim.

---

## 12. CHANGELOG date in auto-generated header is "2026-04-23" — same day as the RC

**Severity: LOW**

The diff shows the new CHANGELOG top-entry as:

> `## [1.0.0](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/v1.0.0-rc.3...v1.0.0) (2026-04-23)`

And the still-present RC entry below as:

> `## [1.0.0-rc.3](…/compare/v0.10.0...v1.0.0-rc.3) (2026-04-23)`

Both dated the same calendar day. Possible (and supported by the audit's timestamps — rc.3 and v1.0.0 are ~hours apart), but: the CHANGELOG format's `(YYYY-MM-DD)` gives zero resolution to distinguish them, so any automated tool that sorts by this date field alone will have to fall back to some other ordering. Not a bug — but the diff doesn't say what ordering is authoritative if a tool consumes this file.

---

## 13. Release body described two different ways

**Severity: MEDIUM**

audit says (launch-audit.md, "GitHub Release v1.0.0" section):

> "Release body: auto-generated by `release.yaml § Generate release notes` from the conventional-commits between `v1.0.0-rc.3` and `v1.0.0` (expected sparse given the narrow commit-range; reconciled CHANGELOG carries the hand-curated v1.0.0 prose separately)."

So the **GitHub Release body is not the CHANGELOG content** — it's auto-generated separately, and the hand-curated prose lives only in the CHANGELOG. That means:

- Visitors to https://github.com/.../releases/tag/v1.0.0 see a (likely empty) auto-gen body
- Visitors to the CHANGELOG see the 50+ lines of hand-curated marketing prose
- npm users see… what? Nothing is said about npm README or package metadata.

This is a **product decision** quietly buried in an audit sentence. The diff treats it as a technicality. A cynical reader flags it because the "First Major Release" marketing prose is exactly what deserves to be on the Release page, and the diff as-written leaves it orphaned.

---

## 14. "Step 27 `Skip PR flow (non-main dispatch ref)` was `skipped` by design" — the boolean logic is stated backward

**Severity: LOW**

> "step 27 `Skip PR flow (non-main dispatch ref)` was `skipped` by design — `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` evaluated true, so the non-main branch is correctly bypassed"

Readable, but grammatically tangled. The step is **named** "Skip PR flow (non-main dispatch ref)" — meaning the step *performs* the skip when not on main. On a main dispatch, the step *itself* is skipped (i.e., the PR flow runs). The prose says `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` evaluated true — so the step's `if:` condition was `if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'`, which evaluated false, and the step was skipped by GitHub Actions. Fine. But the closing phrase "so the non-main branch is correctly bypassed" is a **non-sequitur** — we are on main; there is no non-main branch to bypass. The correct gloss is "the non-main-only PR-skip logic was correctly bypassed." Minor but the kind of sloppy language that hides real defects in similar steps.

---

## 15. Duplicate / inconsistent "Sign-off" text

**Severity: HIGH**

The audit's `## Sign-off` section (edited in the first hunk at line 201 of launch-audit.md) and the audit's new "Decision: v1.0.0 launch status" section (at the bottom, line ~509) **both** quote the sign-off line. They agree — but only because both were written at the same time. The "Decision" section prefaces its blockquote with:

> "The `## Sign-off` section (above) is updated with the dual-sentence form:"

This is documentation that documents itself. If someone edits the `## Sign-off` line later without also updating the "Decision" blockquote, the two will drift silently. The diff creates a **duplicate source of truth** within a single file with no marker to indicate which is canonical. Prefer to delete the blockquote and replace with a cross-reference (`see § Sign-off above`) — but the diff chooses duplication.

---

## 16. Verification block `grep` command quoting is fragile

**Severity: LOW**

RELEASING.md:627:

> `grep -c '^## \[1\.0\.0\] - TBD' CHANGELOG.md    # expected: 0`

The same section immediately above (at dispatch time) also has:

> `grep -cE '^## \[1\.0\.0\]' CHANGELOG.md         # expected: 1`

The first is plain `grep -c` with a literal-dot escape that works under BRE; the second is `grep -cE` (ERE). Mixing BRE and ERE in the same verification block is stylistic noise — worse, it makes copy-paste refactors error-prone. Low severity, but the release-engineering document trades on precision; inconsistent regex modes are exactly the kind of thing that bites on a hurried cut.

---

## 17. "227 files, 1,697,974 bytes unpacked" — no size check against prior release

**Severity: LOW**

The `.dist` JSON is captured verbatim (fileCount: 227, unpackedSize: 1697974) but the audit makes **no comparison** to v1.0.0-rc.3's file count/unpacked size. A one-line delta against the RC would catch a class of supply-chain issues (accidentally bundled dotfile, missing `files:` entry, etc.). The diff records the numbers without interpreting them.

---

## 18. "NFR9: within 1h of publish" — clock starts from a single-second timestamp

**Severity: LOW**

> "NFR9 requires cross-platform install verification (Linux + Windows via the `quality.yaml` matrix + macOS manual spot-check) within 60 minutes of the `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z` publish timestamp."

That gives a deadline of **19:56:39Z**. If Story 5.4 starts "immediately after Story 5.3's Commit 2 … lands on `main`" (per the audit's final sentence), and CHANGELOG reconciliation PRs typically take at least a few minutes to review and merge, the 60-minute envelope is already being eaten by this very document's landing delay. The audit does not budget approval-and-merge time into the NFR9 clock. A cynical reader expects Story 5.4 to arrive claiming "completed at 19:XX:XX, within NFR9 envelope" without noting how much slack was consumed by the sign-off commit itself.

---

## Summary by severity

- **HIGH (3):** Sign-off timestamp precedes the event it documents (#1); duplicate/drift-prone sign-off quoting (#15); contradicting claims about whether approval is required at both gates (#6 — reclassified on reflection, let me pick one — keeping #1 and #15 at HIGH; #6 is MEDIUM).
- **MEDIUM (6):** Stale `§ Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` anchor reference contract (#2); "sparse" vs "EMPTY" release-body/CHANGELOG wording (#3); SLSA L2 claim unsupported by captured evidence (#4); bot-PR admin-bypass-merge race-shape (#5); two-gate vs bypass inconsistency (#6); `npm version major` semver behavior asserted without citation (#7); release body vs CHANGELOG content orphan (#13).
- **LOW (8):** NFR1 arithmetic has 9s unexplained (#8); rolling-10 window cherry-picking (#9); 5 modes vs 14 workflows unreconciled (#10); "14 workflows / 14 knowledge fragments" copy-paste suspicion (#11); same-day CHANGELOG date ambiguity (#12); step-27 prose tangled (#14); BRE/ERE mixing in grep verification (#16); unpacked-size has no delta-vs-RC sanity check (#17); NFR9 clock doesn't budget merge time (#18).

**No findings at CRITICAL.** The diff is a doc-only, post-hoc reconciliation — it has no code, no migration, no schema change. The worst an error here can do is misrepresent an already-committed release. That is MEDIUM/HIGH territory at most, not CRITICAL.

---

## 8. "Zero cache miss" in NFR1 calculation arithmetic

**Severity: LOW**

launch-audit.md NFR1 section:

- Dispatch: `2026-04-23T18:49:47Z`
- Completion: `2026-04-23T18:56:48Z`
- Claim: **7m 1s** wall-clock

18:56:48 − 18:49:47 = **7m 1s** ✓ arithmetic is fine.

- Env-gate approval: "2m 45s" (18:49:47 → 18:52:32)
- 18:52:32 − 18:49:47 = **2m 45s** ✓
- Workflow automation: "4m 16s" (18:52:32 → 18:56:48)
- 18:56:48 − 18:52:32 = **4m 16s** ✓

So the arithmetic is actually consistent — but note: **2m 45s + 4m 16s = 7m 1s** exactly, which assumes the two sub-budgets are contiguous with no gap. The audit presents this as fact without evidence, and the publish-timestamp (18:56:39Z) sits inside the "workflow automation" window. If the workflow truly completed at 18:56:48Z but published at 18:56:39Z, there are **9 seconds of post-publish step execution** unaccounted for. Not wrong, but unexplained in a document that otherwise goes to forensic precision about timestamps.

---

## 9. "Rolling-10-release window" data is cherry-picked

**Severity: LOW**

> "Rolling-10-release window: 2 / 10 data points collected — `[Story 5.2 rc.3: 5m 4s, Story 5.3 v1.0.0: 7m 1s]`."

A rolling-10 window is claimed but the audit drops 8 of 10 slots. Worse — Story 5.2 is described elsewhere in this very diff as **"7 attempts" over "~6 hours"**. Including only the final successful 5m 4s attempt in the NFR1 metric, while the broader narrative leans on the 6-hour cost, is selection bias. NFR1's "20 min (15 CI + 5 approval)" target is trivially met if you count only the successful attempts; if you count all dispatches, rc.3 alone blew past the target ~20x over. The diff does not declare how failed dispatches count toward the rolling window.

---

## 10. Markdown: unclosed table-like list in the "Workflows" section

**Severity: LOW**

CHANGELOG.md "Workflows" table lists **14 workflows** in its rows, matching the bullet "**14 workflows**" above. Good. But the bullet earlier says:

> "**1 agent** — Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian) with 5 workflow-driven modes"

5 modes, 14 workflows. These numbers are not reconciled anywhere in the diff. What is a "mode" vs a "workflow"? Is a mode a subset, superset, or orthogonal concept? The release notes assert both numbers with authority and let the reader guess.

---

## 11. "IDE Support — 23 IDEs" headline number does not match the comma-separated list

**Severity: HIGH**

CHANGELOG.md:55:

> "23 IDEs supported: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, Junie, Kiro, Trae, Google Antigravity, Auggie, CodeBuddy, Crush, iFlow, KiloCoder, Ona, OpenCode, Pi, Qoder, QwenCoder, Rovo Dev"

Count: Claude Code (1), Cursor (2), Windsurf (3), Cline (4), Roo Code (5), GitHub Copilot (6), Codex (7), Gemini CLI (8), Junie (9), Kiro (10), Trae (11), Google Antigravity (12), Auggie (13), CodeBuddy (14), Crush (15), iFlow (16), KiloCoder (17), Ona (18), OpenCode (19), Pi (20), Qoder (21), QwenCoder (22), Rovo Dev (23).

OK — **23 matches**. I was primed to find a mismatch and there isn't one. Finding withdrawn. Replacing with:

**Replacement 11 — Highlights self-contradiction on "14" count**

> "**14 workflows** — full lifecycle from source analysis…"
> "**14 knowledge fragments** — curated cross-cutting principles…"

Two different categories coincidentally both number exactly 14. That's not intrinsically wrong, but the phrasing reuse ("14 X, 14 Y") reads like a template that was partially filled, and a cynical reader will assume at least one of those counts is copy-paste inertia until the diff provides evidence otherwise. The diff provides no enumeration of the 14 knowledge fragments — only the 14 workflows are enumerated in the table below. One of the two "14s" is verifiable from the diff content; the other is an unverified claim.

---

## 12. CHANGELOG date in auto-generated header is "2026-04-23" — same day as the RC

**Severity: LOW**

The diff shows the new CHANGELOG top-entry as:

> `## [1.0.0](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/v1.0.0-rc.3...v1.0.0) (2026-04-23)`

And the still-present RC entry below as:

> `## [1.0.0-rc.3](…/compare/v0.10.0...v1.0.0-rc.3) (2026-04-23)`

Both dated the same calendar day. Possible (and supported by the audit's timestamps — rc.3 and v1.0.0 are ~hours apart), but: the CHANGELOG format's `(YYYY-MM-DD)` gives zero resolution to distinguish them, so any automated tool that sorts by this date field alone will have to fall back to some other ordering. Not a bug — but the diff doesn't say what ordering is authoritative if a tool consumes this file.

---

## 13. Release body described two different ways

**Severity: MEDIUM**

audit says (launch-audit.md, "GitHub Release v1.0.0" section):

> "Release body: auto-generated by `release.yaml § Generate release notes` from the conventional-commits between `v1.0.0-rc.3` and `v1.0.0` (expected sparse given the narrow commit-range; reconciled CHANGELOG carries the hand-curated v1.0.0 prose separately)."

So the **GitHub Release body is not the CHANGELOG content** — it's auto-generated separately, and the hand-curated prose lives only in the CHANGELOG. That means:

- Visitors to https://github.com/.../releases/tag/v1.0.0 see a (likely empty) auto-gen body
- Visitors to the CHANGELOG see the 50+ lines of hand-curated marketing prose
- npm users see… what? Nothing is said about npm README or package metadata.

This is a **product decision** quietly buried in an audit sentence. The diff treats it as a technicality. A cynical reader flags it because the "First Major Release" marketing prose is exactly what deserves to be on the Release page, and the diff as-written leaves it orphaned.

---

## 14. "Step 27 `Skip PR flow (non-main dispatch ref)` was `skipped` by design" — the boolean logic is stated backward

**Severity: LOW**

> "step 27 `Skip PR flow (non-main dispatch ref)` was `skipped` by design — `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` evaluated true, so the non-main branch is correctly bypassed"

Readable, but grammatically tangled. The step is **named** "Skip PR flow (non-main dispatch ref)" — meaning the step *performs* the skip when not on main. On a main dispatch, the step *itself* is skipped (i.e., the PR flow runs). The prose says `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` evaluated true — so the step's `if:` condition was `if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'`, which evaluated false, and the step was skipped by GitHub Actions. Fine. But the closing phrase "so the non-main branch is correctly bypassed" is a **non-sequitur** — we are on main; there is no non-main branch to bypass. The correct gloss is "the non-main-only PR-skip logic was correctly bypassed." Minor but the kind of sloppy language that hides real defects in similar steps.

---

## 15. Duplicate / inconsistent "Sign-off" text

**Severity: HIGH**

The audit's `## Sign-off` section (edited in the first hunk at line 201 of launch-audit.md) and the audit's new "Decision: v1.0.0 launch status" section (at the bottom, line ~509) **both** quote the sign-off line. They agree — but only because both were written at the same time. The "Decision" section prefaces its blockquote with:

> "The `## Sign-off` section (above) is updated with the dual-sentence form:"

This is documentation that documents itself. If someone edits the `## Sign-off` line later without also updating the "Decision" blockquote, the two will drift silently. The diff creates a **duplicate source of truth** within a single file with no marker to indicate which is canonical. Prefer to delete the blockquote and replace with a cross-reference (`see § Sign-off above`) — but the diff chooses duplication.

---

## 16. Verification block `grep` command quoting is fragile

**Severity: LOW**

RELEASING.md:627:

> `grep -c '^## \[1\.0\.0\] - TBD' CHANGELOG.md    # expected: 0`

The same section immediately above (at dispatch time) also has:

> `grep -cE '^## \[1\.0\.0\]' CHANGELOG.md         # expected: 1`

The first is plain `grep -c` with a literal-dot escape that works under BRE; the second is `grep -cE` (ERE). Mixing BRE and ERE in the same verification block is stylistic noise — worse, it makes copy-paste refactors error-prone. Low severity, but the release-engineering document trades on precision; inconsistent regex modes are exactly the kind of thing that bites on a hurried cut.

---

## 17. "227 files, 1,697,974 bytes unpacked" — no size check against prior release

**Severity: LOW**

The `.dist` JSON is captured verbatim (fileCount: 227, unpackedSize: 1697974) but the audit makes **no comparison** to v1.0.0-rc.3's file count/unpacked size. A one-line delta against the RC would catch a class of supply-chain issues (accidentally bundled dotfile, missing `files:` entry, etc.). The diff records the numbers without interpreting them.

---

## 18. "NFR9: within 1h of publish" — clock starts from a single-second timestamp

**Severity: LOW**

> "NFR9 requires cross-platform install verification (Linux + Windows via the `quality.yaml` matrix + macOS manual spot-check) within 60 minutes of the `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z` publish timestamp."

That gives a deadline of **19:56:39Z**. If Story 5.4 starts "immediately after Story 5.3's Commit 2 … lands on `main`" (per the audit's final sentence), and CHANGELOG reconciliation PRs typically take at least a few minutes to review and merge, the 60-minute envelope is already being eaten by this very document's landing delay. The audit does not budget approval-and-merge time into the NFR9 clock. A cynical reader expects Story 5.4 to arrive claiming "completed at 19:XX:XX, within NFR9 envelope" without noting how much slack was consumed by the sign-off commit itself.

---

## Summary by severity

- **HIGH (3):** Sign-off timestamp precedes the event it documents (#1); duplicate/drift-prone sign-off quoting (#15); contradicting claims about whether approval is required at both gates (#6 — reclassified on reflection, let me pick one — keeping #1 and #15 at HIGH; #6 is MEDIUM).
- **MEDIUM (6):** Stale `§ Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` anchor reference contract (#2); "sparse" vs "EMPTY" release-body/CHANGELOG wording (#3); SLSA L2 claim unsupported by captured evidence (#4); bot-PR admin-bypass-merge race-shape (#5); two-gate vs bypass inconsistency (#6); `npm version major` semver behavior asserted without citation (#7); release body vs CHANGELOG content orphan (#13).
- **LOW (8):** NFR1 arithmetic has 9s unexplained (#8); rolling-10 window cherry-picking (#9); 5 modes vs 14 workflows unreconciled (#10); "14 workflows / 14 knowledge fragments" copy-paste suspicion (#11); same-day CHANGELOG date ambiguity (#12); step-27 prose tangled (#14); BRE/ERE mixing in grep verification (#16); unpacked-size has no delta-vs-RC sanity check (#17); NFR9 clock doesn't budget merge time (#18).

**No findings at CRITICAL.** The diff is a doc-only, post-hoc reconciliation — it has no code, no migration, no schema change. The worst an error here can do is misrepresent an already-committed release. That is MEDIUM/HIGH territory at most, not CRITICAL.

All sub-sections are properly populated. Now I have enough data to produce my findings.

# Acceptance Audit — Story 5.3

## Priority-AC verdicts

- **AC 1b (Commit 2 subject + exactly 3-file scope):** **PASS**. Commit `c0b2b67` subject is exactly the prescribed `docs(release): reconcile CHANGELOG v1.0.0 and sign off publication (Story 5.3)`, and `git diff-tree` shows exactly three `M` entries: `CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/RELEASING.md`, `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` — no additions, no extras.

- **AC 10 (CHANGELOG reconciliation):** **PASS**. Diff shows the auto-gen header at line 8 preserved verbatim (`## [1.0.0](.../compare/v1.0.0-rc.3...v1.0.0) (2026-04-23)`), hand-curated prose grafted directly beneath it, and block B (the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` placeholder + HTML comment + body) deleted entirely (diff lines 70–129). Post-edit greps confirm: `^## \[1\.0\.0\]`=1, `- TBD`=0, `\(https`=1, `1.0.0-rc.3`=1, `0.10.0`=1 — all match AC 10 (iii) exactly.

- **AC 11 (Audit append — 9 sub-sections + Sign-off additive append):** **PASS**. New `## Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut` H2 at line 419 with all 9 prescribed sub-sections in order: Workflow dispatch run, Bot PR auto-merge evidence, npm publish evidence, GitHub Release v1.0.0, Main-branch side-effects, CHANGELOG reconciliation, NFR1 cycle-time record, NFR6 v1.0.0 immutability activation, Decision: v1.0.0 launch status. Sign-off at line 201 preserves the Story 5.2 sentence and adds `v1.0.0 launched 2026-04-23 18:56:39Z; Story 5.4 cross-platform verification in scope.` as an additive append.

- **AC 14 (RELEASING.md section flip, five-element shape):** **PASS**. `### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest` at line 559 with all five elements present (Trigger / CLI / Expected outcome / Constraints / Verification). Old `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` removed (count=0). The Story 3.4 appendix (`#### Story 3.4 refactor note — PR-auto-merge flow`) is absorbed into the main section body (count=0), matching AC 14 (e).

- **AC 15 (Commit hygiene):** **PASS with one inherited-from-spec concern** (see Finding 1 below). Body has workflow URL, bot PR URL + merge SHA `393a5a2`, Release URL, the 3-file list with per-file purpose one-liners, a dedicated CHANGELOG reconciliation decision paragraph, no `Fixes #NN` trailer, no `_bmad-output/todo/` references, and `AC N` reference form (`AC 1b / AC 10 / AC 11 / AC 14 / AC 18`). PR references `#213 / #205 / #207` are inline URLs/context, not `Fixes` trailers — compliant.

- **AC 18 (NFR6 immutability timestamp):** **PASS**. Timestamp `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z` appears verbatim in four locations in the audit artifact (lines 421, 644, 646, 656), with the prescribed canonical sentence at line 644 matching AC 11 (viii) word-for-word.

- **AC 20 (Zero new files):** **PASS**. `git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-status` shows only `M` entries; zero `A` (added) entries.

## Findings

### 1. Commit-subject char-count contradicts its own AC annotation
- **AC:** AC 15 (minor; inherited from spec)
- **Evidence:** `git log -1 --format='%s' c0b2b67 | wc -c` yields **78 chars**; AC 15 prescribes the exact subject string `docs(release): reconcile CHANGELOG v1.0.0 and sign off publication (Story 5.3)` while parenthetically asserting `(64 chars — under the 72-char limit)` — both claims in the spec are wrong (it's 78 chars, above the conventional 72 limit). The implementer used the exact string the AC prescribed verbatim, so the deviation is spec-internal, not an implementation defect. No commit-msg hook caught it (commitlint likely set to a higher limit or no subject-length rule).
- **Severity:** LOW — implementation matches AC text literally; the miscount is a spec defect to note for Epic 5 retrospective, not a Story 5.3 violation.

### 2. Zero-conventional-commit edge case handled correctly, but its interaction with AC 10 wording is worth flagging
- **AC:** AC 10 (observational, not a violation)
- **Evidence:** `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:417` documents that the auto-generated `## [1.0.0]` block had an EMPTY body (no `### Features` / `### Bug Fixes`) because the `v1.0.0-rc.3..v1.0.0` commit range contained only `docs(release):` and `chore(release):` entries. AC 10 protocol (i) says "REPLACE the auto-generated block's BODY (the `### Features`/`### Bug Fixes` sub-sections) with the hand-curated prose" — with an empty body, there was nothing to replace, only to inject. Task 4 subtask at spec line 114 pre-acknowledges this ("body was EMPTY per zero-conventional-commit edge case; reconciliation simply injects hand-curated prose"). Outcome is correct.
- **Severity:** LOW — no violation; annotating for retrospective so future reconciliations know the branch behavior is exercised.

### 3. `git log main --grep='Story 5.3'` returns only one commit, not two as Task 8 implies
- **AC:** AC 1 / AC 17 (minor accuracy)
- **Evidence:** Spec Task 8 subtask (line 156) says `git log main --grep='Story 5.3' --oneline` "returns Commit 2 (`c0b2b67`) + merge commit `8e0919f` (merge body inherits PR-commit-list via `gh pr merge --admin` so matches 'Story 5.3' via the referenced commit)". Actual output returns `8e0919f Merge pull request #214` + `c0b2b67 docs(release): reconcile CHANGELOG v1.0.0 and sign off publication (Story 5.3)` — the merge commit subject `Merge pull request #214 from armelhbobdad/feat/v1-final-signoff` does NOT literally contain "Story 5.3" in its subject line, but `--grep` matches against the commit message (subject + body), and the merge body on GitHub's default merge likely includes the PR title/body which references Story 5.3. The Task 8 assertion is correct as observed.
- **Severity:** LOW / no-op — verification passed as described.

### 4. `## Sign-off` inline-edit rather than block-append — compliant but note style
- **AC:** AC 11 (Sign-off additive append)
- **Evidence:** AC 11 says to "append a second sentence" to the existing `## Sign-off` paragraph. The diff at `v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:201` shows the edit appended the new sentence to the SAME physical line rather than starting a new line/paragraph: `... Story 5.3 unblocked. v1.0.0 launched 2026-04-23 18:56:39Z; Story 5.4 cross-platform verification in scope.` AC wording "append a second sentence" allows this form (sentence-append, not paragraph-append), so this passes. Worth flagging only in case the reviewer prefers a paragraph break.
- **Severity:** LOW / stylistic — compliant with literal AC text.

### 5. All other priority ACs clean
- AC 1b, AC 10, AC 11 (all 9 sub-sections + Sign-off), AC 14 (five-element shape + appendix absorption), AC 15 (body requirements a–i), AC 18 (NFR6 timestamp verbatim), AC 20 (zero new files) — all verified clean by direct grep/count/file inspection.

## Summary

No CRITICAL, HIGH, or MEDIUM violations. The Commit 2 scope envelope, CHANGELOG reconciliation protocol, 9-sub-section audit append, docs/RELEASING.md section flip, NFR6 immutability timestamp, and zero-new-files constraint all comply literally with the spec. The only notable item is spec-internal (AC 15 parenthetical miscounts the subject length as 64 chars when the prescribed string is actually 78 chars); the implementer used the spec's verbatim subject string, so this is a spec defect to note for Epic 5 retrospective rather than a Story 5.3 violation.

Relevant files reviewed:
- `/tmp/story-5-3-diff.patch`
- `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/CHANGELOG.md`
- `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/docs/RELEASING.md`
- `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`
- `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/5-3-cut-v1-0-0-under-tag-latest-with-manual-approval.md`

AC 10 protocol (i) says "REPLACE the auto-generated block's BODY (the `### Features`/`### Bug Fixes` sub-sections) with the hand-curated prose" — with an empty body, there was nothing to replace, only to inject. Task 4 subtask at spec line 114 pre-acknowledges this ("body was EMPTY per zero-conventional-commit edge case; reconciliation simply injects hand-curated prose"). Outcome is correct.
- **Severity:** LOW — no violation; annotating for retrospective so future reconciliations know the branch behavior is exercised.

### 3. `git log main --grep='Story 5.3'` returns only one commit, not two as Task 8 implies
- **AC:** AC 1 / AC 17 (minor accuracy)
- **Evidence:** Spec Task 8 subtask (line 156) says `git log main --grep='Story 5.3' --oneline` "returns Commit 2 (`c0b2b67`) + merge commit `8e0919f` (merge body inherits PR-commit-list via `gh pr merge --admin` so matches 'Story 5.3' via the referenced commit)". Actual output returns `8e0919f Merge pull request #214` + `c0b2b67 docs(release): reconcile CHANGELOG v1.0.0 and sign off publication (Story 5.3)` — the merge commit subject `Merge pull request #214 from armelhbobdad/feat/v1-final-signoff` does NOT literally contain "Story 5.3" in its subject line, but `--grep` matches against the commit message (subject + body), and the merge body on GitHub's default merge likely includes the PR title/body which references Story 5.3. The Task 8 assertion is correct as observed.
- **Severity:** LOW / no-op — verification passed as described.

### 4. `## Sign-off` inline-edit rather than block-append — compliant but note style
- **AC:** AC 11 (Sign-off additive append)
- **Evidence:** AC 11 says to "append a second sentence" to the existing `## Sign-off` paragraph. The diff at `v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:201` shows the edit appended the new sentence to the SAME physical line rather than starting a new line/paragraph: `... Story 5.3 unblocked. v1.0.0 launched 2026-04-23 18:56:39Z; Story 5.4 cross-platform verification in scope.` AC wording "append a second sentence" allows this form (sentence-append, not paragraph-append), so this passes. Worth flagging only in case the reviewer prefers a paragraph break.
- **Severity:** LOW / stylistic — compliant with literal AC text.

### 5. All other priority ACs clean
- AC 1b, AC 10, AC 11 (all 9 sub-sections + Sign-off), AC 14 (five-element shape + appendix absorption), AC 15 (body requirements a–i), AC 18 (NFR6 timestamp verbatim), AC 20 (zero new files) — all verified clean by direct grep/count/file inspection.

## Summary

No CRITICAL, HIGH, or MEDIUM violations. The Commit 2 scope envelope, CHANGELOG reconciliation protocol, 9-sub-section audit append, docs/RELEASING.md section flip, NFR6 immutability timestamp, and zero-new-files constraint all comply literally with the spec. The only notable item is spec-internal (AC 15 parenthetical miscounts the subject length as 64 chars when the prescribed string is actually 78 chars); the implementer used the spec's verbatim subject string, so this is a spec defect to note for Epic 5 retrospective rather than a Story 5.3 violation.
